findings.issue: callGoal success tests (ExecutesGoalAndInsertsResult, SuccessWritesValueToOutput) assert DoesNotContain('[Error:') but don't verify the actual goal output appears in the rendered string. If callGoal silently returned empty string, these tests pass.
findings.impact: Low — the success code path IS exercised (coverage confirms lines 201-204 hit), so if callGoal wrote nothing, the variable.set step would still run. The assertion gap is narrow.
findings.suggestion: Add assertion like Contains('Hello from goal') or Contains('42') to verify the actual value is written to template output.

findings.issue: Render.Run() still at 0% — all tests call _provider.Render(action) directly.
findings.impact: Low — Run() is a one-liner delegation. If [Provider] resolution fails, no C# test catches it, but this would surface in PLang integration tests.
findings.suggestion: Not blocking. PLang tests will cover this when built.

findings.issue: IOException catch (lines 48-50) and callGoal empty-name path (lines 193-195) remain uncovered. These are defensive code paths.
findings.impact: Low — these are safety nets that only trigger under unusual conditions (permission denied, corrupted template tag).
findings.suggestion: Not blocking. Could add chmod-based IOException test for completeness.

summary: All 1890 C# tests pass (4 new tests added). All 3 major findings from v1 resolved: callGoal tests now use real goals (branch coverage 37.5% → 68.8%), LooksLikeFilePath now covered via 3 auto-detect tests, GetTemplateBaseDir goal-relative path covered. FluidProvider overall at 91.8% line / 89.1% branch. 3 minor findings remain (weak callGoal output assertions, Run() bypass, defensive code paths). Approved for security analysis.
